summary: Next 12 Biggest Wins is materially closed as the latest recommended closeout wave; journey proof is ready; flagship replacement readiness is still open; overall progress is 100% in 'Public-fit polish'; the longest pole remains Core Engine; support closure is clear.

release_health.reason: Published flagship readiness proof is not green, so release posture cannot overclaim even when red blockers are clear.

flagship_readiness.reason: Published flagship readiness proof is fail. Coverage gaps remain: desktop_client, horizons_and_public_surface. Readiness-plane gaps remain: flagship_ready, veteran_deep_workflow_ready, dense_workbench_ready, recovery_trust_ready.

top_support_or_feedback_clusters.summary: Next 12 Biggest Wins is the active pressure cluster: finish install-specific trust/support truth, creator publication and shelf posture, pulse-v3 launch governance, and no-step-back utility parity.
Downloads, updates, support closure, and channel-aware trust copy now exist as first-party surfaces and must keep moving in lockstep instead of drifting back into separate promises.
The current longest pole is Core Engine, so release, support, and publication decisions should assume that this lane still sets the pacing risk for the broader public product.

governor_decisions.reason: Keep delivery focus on Next 12 Biggest Wins. The pulse shows 100% overall progress in 'Public-fit polish', history depth has reached 40 snapshots, and the pacing risk remains concentrated in Core Engine while trust/publication/utility closure still needs measured completion on the active registry.
Freeze launch expansion until published flagship readiness proof returns to ready posture.
